prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: condensed Fukui functions (f+, f–, f0, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic (f+) and most nucleophilic (f–) atomic sites for benzaldehyde at its supplied geometry.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting), as implemented in PySCF 2.x via chemkit’s fukui-reactivity skill.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization performed; property evaluated on that fixed geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Total molecular charge: 0; spin multiplicity: 1 (closed-shell singlet). N–1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states were treated as doublets (multiplicity = 2) in the finite-difference cycles.
Software / version: chemkit-MCP wrapper calling PySCF (DFT SCF) for the neutral, cationic, and anionic single-point energies/charges.
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.  The engine reported three warnings (verbatim below).  No fall-backs triggered.
Live log: the run was streamed to a live log file announced at launch (path reprinted in the CLI line below); all SCF details can be inspected there.
Command (from cli_invocation):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout path /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpu_3tpuis/live.json

Key numerical result (highest electrophilic susceptibility)
Most electrophilic atom: C3 (index 2) with f⁺ = 0.39938968 (dimensionless)

Engine warnings (verbatim)
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. schema_header present
